purpose: Pick from a fixed set of options: a light-DOM element wrapping a real <select> inside its own label. Authored option content is adopted into the select; remaining default children are the visible label.
useWhen: The user picks one option from a fixed list too long to lay out as radios.
Space is constrained and the options can collapse behind a field.
Several options may be picked from one list — `multiple` renders a native list box.

notWhen.case: A handful of options that should all be visible at once.

guidance: Options are real <option>/<optgroup>/<hr> children and keep their native semantics — the component adopts them into the internal select untouched.
Leave `value` unset for uncontrolled behavior (the platform selects the first option); include an empty-value "Choose…" option when no default should win.

default.description: <option>/<optgroup>/<hr> children populate the select; everything else is the visible label content (the accessible name via the wrapping label).

prefix.description: Affix content at the field's inline start — any markup: svg, icon-font glyph, HTML character, image.
guidance: Affixes are aria-hidden: information an affix carries must also live in the field's accessible name or description.

suffix.description: Affix content at the field's inline end; replaces the drawn chevron indicator.
guidance: Supplying a suffix takes over the open-indicator role — make it still read as "this opens".

keyboard.does: Moves through the options (native select behavior, preserved).
Opens the platform picker; details vary by OS (native).
Native typeahead jumps to the first matching option.

examples.title: Custom open indicator
examples.why: A suffix replaces the drawn chevron; it is aria-hidden, so it stays purely visual.

examples.title: Placeholder option as label
examples.why: A disappearing first option is not an accessible name. Give the field label children or aria-label.
